column1: # All tests done on Thinkpad P14s Gen 5
# All fans on maximum for duration of test
# First tests done on Test Order 15 (15 boxes)
# Second batch done on Test Order 25 (25 boxes)

column16: "Extremely slight improvement due to high score usually being found fast enough without warm start"
"No improvements due to bounding rule 2 never triggering."
"No improvement due to rule 2 still not being aggresive enough."
"Rule 4 does a magnificent job of pruning branches early
column17: leading to a faster finish even with almost a third slower nodes per second processing speed."

column16: "Rule 2 update has still not made it fire once."
"An incredible optimization. Now to check whether the results are much more unoptimal or not."

"Another breakthrough! Turns out that testing a bunch of randomly generated boxes leads you to the understanding that having a bunch of the same box dimension leads to combinatorial explosions and deduplication / symmetry breakage must occur for computation timeframes to be reasonable

column17: but performance benchmarks from here on out will be done on test order 25 (25 boxes) due to achieving trivialization of test order 15."

column16: "Shame that such a smart bound on paper results in neglegible pruning
column17: at the cost of so much performance. This rule can go immediately."

column16: "A small problem where things were doubly calculated was fixed along with the deprecation of the heightmap recognition rule."
"Failed experiment. Turns out the existing extreme point logic already took care of most of what this rule aimed to do."
